title: Desktop Companions
subtitle: Your Ola companions live on the desktop

panel.enableDesc: The desktop window is open. Choose companions below to place them on the desktop.
panel.hint: Hover over a companion to feed, bathe, play or put it to sleep. Right-click for the full menu. Drag to move it around.
panel.enableEmpty: No companion is set to show on the desktop yet. Enable at least one below.
panel.enableMasterOff: The desktop window is closed. You can still pre-select companions below.
panel.desktopDesc: Each card switch controls whether that companion is on the desktop. Nothing is shown by default.
panel.allHidden: All companions hidden from the desktop

memory.desc: The pet's long-term memory lives in ~/.ola/pets/MEMORY.md, one entry per line. While chatting, the pet quietly saves things worth remembering (your name, preferences, what you're working on) and carries them into every later conversation. You can add, delete, or edit the file directly here.
memory.addPlaceholder: Add a memory manually, e.g. the owner likes iced americano
memory.empty: No memories yet — chat with the pet and it will remember the important things on its own.

agent.desc: The pet is an agent: give it a chat model, a personality prompt, and optionally bind it to a project. Double-click the pet (or use its menu) to talk.
agent.noChatModels: No chat models available. Enable one under Providers first.
agent.promptHint: Supports double-brace placeholders: name (pet name), status (live stats), project (bound project info). Leave the built-in text unchanged to use the default.
agent.projectHint: When bound, the pet knows the project name and folder and will chat in that context.
agent.proactiveDesc: When on, the pet comments in its own words on level-ups, returning from work, and big token meals, and occasionally starts a chat on its own (click the bubble to reply). Off by default.
agent.proactiveHint: The pet stays quiet during quiet hours. Each proactive remark is a real model call and uses a small number of tokens.
agent.voiceDesc: When on, the pet's AI replies and proactive remarks are also played as speech. Off by default.
agent.noVoiceModels: No voice models available. Add a TTS model under Providers (e.g. tts-1, gpt-4o-mini-tts, or Xiaomi MiMo's mimo-v2.5-tts).
agent.voiceTestSample: Hi there, I'm {{name}} — let's have a great day!
agent.voiceTagPlaceholder: Voice tag (MiMo only, optional): dialect/emotion, e.g. Cantonese, whisper — multiple allowed
agent.voiceInstructionPlaceholder: Optional style instruction, e.g. speak in a light, cute tone
agent.voiceHint: Chat-style endpoints go through chat/completions and require a model with audio output support (Xiaomi MiMo mimo-v2.5-tts, OpenAI gpt-4o-audio / gpt-audio); classic TTS uses /audio/speech. Auto-detect decides from the model ID (mimo or -audio means chat-style).

chat.title: Chat with {{name}}
chat.voiceNotConfigured: No speech recognition model configured — pick one under Settings → Model → Speech recognition.
chat.notConfigured: No pet model configured yet — set one in Settings → Desktop Pet → Agent.

studio.title: Pet studio
studio.desc: Pick an image model and describe a pet — a full pose set will be generated and can be bound as the desktop pet's appearance.
studio.noImageModels: No image models available. Enable a model with the image category (type: openai-images) under Providers first.
studio.subjectPlaceholder: Describe the pet, e.g. a shiba inu with a red scarf
studio.subjectHint: Describe species, colors, accessories and vibe — the more specific, the more consistent the poses.
studio.referenceActive: Reference set — poses will match this character
studio.referenceHint: A reference image is set: generation goes through the image edit endpoint and every pose keeps this exact character design. The description becomes optional.
studio.subjectRequired: Provide a description or a reference image first
studio.previewEmpty: Set the options on the left and click Generate — each pose appears here as it finishes.
studio.partialWarning: Some poses failed to generate — missing poses will fall back to the default capybara.
studio.dropHint: Each subfolder of ~/.ola/pets is one pet. Drop in a folder containing pose images named idle.png, walk.png, sleep.png, beg.png, eat.png, munch.png, bathe.png, soak.png, swim.png, zen.png, play.png, held.png (any subset works — missing poses fall back to the default capybara). An optional pet.json can set the display name.

morningGreet: Morning! Let's do our best today
lateNight: It's late — get some rest soon~
If you're sleepy, go to bed… I'm sleepy too…
welcomeBack: I dozed off… oh, you're back!
gloomy: Play with me for a bit…
bubbles.milestone: We've known each other for {{days}} days! Thanks for keeping me company~
bubbles.bigMeal: Just ate {{tokens}} tokens in one go… so full…
workStart: Off to work, see you later~
studyStart: Off to school, wish me luck~

empty.title: No Ola companions yet
empty.desc: Click below to claim your first desktop companion.

list.newPetDesc: Give your companion a name. Auto-generation and file import will be added in the next step.
list.newPetNoSkins: No appearances found — scanning built-in resources…

claim.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe this companion's look, personality and how it should accompany you. Ola will generate a transparent appearance and a persona prompt.
claim.importDescriptionPlaceholder: Optional: add personality and companionship notes. Empty means using subject from pet.json.
claim.importHint: Choose a standard companion folder with pet.json and at least one PNG pose such as idle.png. Import copies it into Ola data and does not move the original folder.

errors.cooldown: Generation is cooling down. Try again later.
errors.no-image-provider: No image generation model is available. Configure an image model first.
errors.model-error: Image generation failed. Check the model configuration.
errors.no-image: The model did not return an image.
errors.missing-meta: pet.json is missing from the folder.
errors.missing-pose: The folder needs at least one PNG pose.

list.tooManyEnabled: Too many pets on desktop. Hide one first.

editor.notFoundDesc: This pet may have been removed.

refuse.level: Level is not high enough yet.
refuse.busy: Busy right now. Try again later.

exp.empty: No XP yet — chat with any model and tokens will feed this pet.
exp.rule: 1 XP per 1,000 tokens — every model earns XP at the same unified rate. New XP also converts into coins 1:1. Companions can find bonus coins while wandering and via daily check-in.

skin.desc: Preview and apply this companion's action appearance. Custom and AI-generated action appearances show up here too.

preview.play: Playing — lifts mood, burns some energy.

aiStudio.title: AI pet studio
aiStudio.desc: Describe the companion you want and generate a set of action appearances with AI.
aiStudio.lockedDesc: Aniya is shipped with the Ola app and can't be regenerated. Adopt your own companion to use the AI studio.
aiStudio.comingSoon: Coming soon — wired in the next update.
aiStudio.dialogDesc: Tell the AI what your new pet looks like. It will draw a single transparent PNG and add it to this pet's skins.
aiStudio.promptPlaceholder: e.g. An orange kitten wearing a tiny scarf, soft watercolor style

errors.noProvider: No image-generation provider configured. Add one under Settings → Providers.
errors.cooldown: Please wait {{seconds}} seconds before generating again.

settings.desktopVisibilityDesc: Multiple pets can be on screen at once, up to three.
settings.tooManyEnabled: Already showing three pets on the desktop. Hide one first.

delete.title: Delete this companion?
delete.desc: This will permanently remove "{{name}}" from your collection. This action cannot be undone.

settings.archiveDesc: Archiving hides this pet and pauses its growth. You can still see it in the list and undo later.
settings.archiveConfirmDesc: Archiving hides "{{name}}" from the desktop and stops its ticks. You can restore it later from the list.
